title: Aster limnophilus
taxonomy: Aster limnophilus is classified within the family Asteraceae, which is commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that includes over 23,000 species. Aster limnophilus is further classified within the tribe Astereae and the genus Aster, which is a group of flowering plants native to temperate regions. The genus Aster is often divided into several sections, but the exact classification of Aster limnophilus is not well-defined. Further research is needed to determine the specific classification of this species.

intro: Aster limnophilus, also known as Marsh Aster or Marsh Blue Aster, is a perennial plant that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Aster limnophilus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal preparations, Aster limnophilus is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create remedies for colds, flu, and respiratory issues.
